deep_dive: Source leads point to a Ming campaign against Bunyashiri near the Onon River on June 15, 1410, but the retrievable exact-date pages are weak derivative references. This record should use a scholarly Ming history source before returning to the visible feed.
why_it_matters: Holding the record avoids presenting a medieval military claim as settled when the current source support is not strong enough for a visible history feed.

title: Fiume Annexation Date Needs Review
one_liner: The Fiume annexation record is held because stronger sources support the Treaty of Rome on January 27 or annexation ceremonies on March 16, not March 3.
description: The Fiume annexation record is held because stronger sources support the Treaty of Rome on January 27 or annexation ceremonies on March 16, not March 3.
short_summary: The Fiume annexation record is held pending a date correction.
summary: The Fiume annexation record is held because stronger sources support the Treaty of Rome on January 27 or annexation ceremonies on March 16, not March 3. The record should be moved or rewritten before returning to the visible feed.
detail: The Fiume annexation record is held because stronger sources support the Treaty of Rome on January 27 or annexation ceremonies on March 16, not March 3. The record should be moved or rewritten before returning to the visible feed.
deep_dive: Italian legal and encyclopedia sources point to March 16, 1924 for civil celebrations and the Fiume provincial designation, while treaty sources point to January 27, 1924 for the Treaty of Rome. That conflicts with the current March 3 placement.
why_it_matters: The date distinction matters because treaty signing, legal celebration, and annexation framing are different political events.

title: Hungary Noble-Ranks Date Needs Review

detail: Official Hungarian legal texts support Act IV of 1947 taking effect on January 14, 1947, abolishing noble and aristocratic ranks and certain titles. This February 14 record is hidden until the date can be corrected or separately sourced.
deep_dive: Hungary Act IV of 1947 abolished noble and aristocratic ranks and certain titles. The official legal record gives January 14, 1947 as the promulgation and effective date, so the current February 14 slot is not safe for the visible feed.
why_it_matters: The hold prevents a legal-history milestone from shipping on a date the official law text does not support.

title: Pan Am Round-the-World Ticket Date Needs Review
one_liner: The January 6 ticket claim is held because stronger Pan Am history sources point to June 17, 1947 for the first regular round-the-world service.
description: The January 6 ticket claim is held because stronger Pan Am history sources point to June 17, 1947 for the first regular round-the-world service.
short_summary: Pan Am round-the-world ticket record held pending stronger exact-date source support.
summary: Date-history pages place a Pan Am round-the-world ticket claim on January 6, 1947, but stronger Pan Am history sources point to June 17, 1947 for the first regular round-the-world service. The record is hidden until the January ticket-offer claim can be verified or moved.
detail: Date-history pages place a Pan Am round-the-world ticket claim on January 6, 1947, but stronger Pan Am history sources point to June 17, 1947 for the first regular round-the-world service. The record is hidden until the January ticket-offer claim can be verified or moved.
deep_dive: Pan Am Historical Foundation materials say Juan Trippe opened new round-the-world schedules on June 17, 1947, and later coverage treats that date as the first regular round-the-world passenger service. That conflict makes the January 6 ticket wording unsafe for the visible feed.
why_it_matters: Holding the record protects a travel milestone from being displayed on a date that stronger aviation sources may contradict.
